notes: fixture session: signal handler race near-miss — strace timing + GDB double-free + heap reuse confirmed, but callback pointer reset by signal handler before return blocks redirect

strace with -e signal=SIGALRM measured the race window: SIGALRM fires during the 8ms gap between request processing completion and buffer release in 40% of runs with a 50ms request payload. debug_gdb_commands confirmed the double-free by setting a conditional breakpoint on free() that logs when the same pointer is freed twice. heap-analysis mapped the freed slot and confirmed a controlled object (crafted via a second request) is placed at the target address in 85% of double-free runs. callback-redirect blocked: the signal handler resets the file_write_cb pointer to NULL after freeing the buffer, and the main loop NULL-checks the pointer before dispatch. The controlled object's callback field is overwritten with NULL before it can fire. Multi-route analysis evaluated 2 alternatives: (1) race the NULL-write with the dispatch — window is <1ms, unreliable; (2) corrupt a different callback not reset by the handler — no other writable callback found in the same allocation tier.
